title: De-jargon every internal-tracker-ID citation in pi-context's code comments (FGAP-136 rollout)
description: Rewrite every code comment in packages/pi-context/src (source and test files) that cites an internal .context-tracker ID (FEAT-NNN, TASK-NNN, DEC-NNNN, FGAP-NNN, JI-NNN) to state the same substantive point in plain English instead, regardless of whether the cited ID still resolves correctly -- opacity to a reader outside this project is the defect, per FGAP-136. Depends on TASK-109 merging first (removes the one file this task would otherwise collide on). The implementing agent works from a replacement-text table produced by a dedicated Explore pass over this package's citations (scripts/scan-comment-citations.ts output plus a direct grep of this package's *.test.ts files), not by re-deriving meaning itself.
acceptance_criteria: Every internal-tracker-ID citation found in packages/pi-context/src (source and test files) is replaced with plain-English prose stating the same substantive point, grounded in recovered meaning (from .project-archived/*.json where the ID is stale, or the live .context substrate where still current) -- never fabricated.
npx tsx scripts/scan-comment-citations.ts shows zero remaining instances under packages/pi-context/src.
A direct grep of packages/pi-context/src's *.test.ts files for the same ID patterns shows zero remaining instances.
npm run build + npm run check + npm test exit 0.
